subject: Criminology Basics
description: Criminal law, statutes, and law enforcement fundamentals.

questions.explanation: RA 9165 (2002) is the Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act.

questions.q: Which law created the Philippine National Police?
questions.explanation: RA 6975 (1990), the DILG Act, established the PNP.

questions.q: Crimes that are wrong because they are prohibited by law, not inherently evil, are called:
questions.explanation: Mala prohibita are wrong only because a statute forbids them; mala in se are inherently immoral.

questions.q: The body of the crime, or the fact that a crime was actually committed, is the:
questions.explanation: Corpus delicti literally means the body of the crime.

questions.q: Under the Revised Penal Code, criminal liability is incurred by any person committing a felony although the wrongful act be different from that intended. This illustrates:
questions.explanation: All three doctrines fall under Art. 4 — liability despite a result different from intent.

questions.q: The scientific study of crime, criminals, and criminal behavior is:
questions.explanation: Criminology studies crime and criminal behavior; criminalistics applies science to evidence.

questions.q: Who is regarded as the father of modern criminology?
questions.explanation: Lombroso, for his positivist studies of the criminal man; Beccaria founded the classical school.

options: PNP Reform and Reorganization Act of 1998
questions.explanation: RA 8551 (1998) reformed and reorganized the PNP.

questions.q: A police officer may arrest without a warrant when:
options: The person is about to commit, is committing, or has just committed an offense in his presence
He suspects the person from rumors
questions.explanation: In flagrante delicto arrest is a recognized warrantless arrest under Rule 113.

questions.q: The study of punishment and prison management is called:
questions.explanation: Penology deals with punishment and correctional administration.
